beats.narration: Every ultrasonic test starts here: sound is mechanical vibration handed particle to particle. The energy travels, the particles barely move.

payload.title: WAVE MODES
payload.sub: How sound travels through solids in four distinct wave modes.

beats.narration: Entry geometry picks the mode: straight in is longitudinal, a tilted wedge makes shear, steeper still rides the surface, thin plates flex.
Watch the particles: longitudinal squeezes them parallel to travel, compressions and rarefactions. Shear shakes them sideways, like a rope. Liquids and gases can't carry it.
In steel, longitudinal runs five thousand nine hundred twenty meters per second. Shear is about half. Surface rides just under shear. Plate varies.
Lock in the rule: velocity belongs to the material and the mode, never the probe. Change the mode and the physics changes with it.

payload.sub: Change the mode and the physics changes with it.
